aids.title: OBST Tables: CWR
aids.content: C = Cost table (c[i][j] = minimum search cost), W = Weight table (w[i][j] = total probability), R = Root table (r[i][j] = optimal root index). Fill W first, then C and R together.
aids.forRemembering: The three tables computed in the OBST dynamic programming algorithm

aids.title: OBST as a Library Shelf Arrangement
aids.content: Imagine arranging books on a shelf where popular books should be at eye level (root). An OBST is like organizing a library where the most-requested books are placed at the most accessible positions, and rarely-requested books are placed further away. The DP algorithm tries every possible arrangement to find the one with the least total walking distance.
aids.forRemembering: Why frequently accessed keys should be near the root in an OBST

aids.title: Filling the DP Table Diagonally
aids.content: The cost table c[i][j] is filled diagonal by diagonal, starting with chains of length 0 (base case c[i][i] = q_i), then length 1, then length 2, etc. Each cell tries all possible roots in its range. Think of it as solving smaller subproblems before larger ones.
aids.forRemembering: The order of computation in the OBST DP algorithm
